title: What Are The Medicinal Properties of Ammi visnaga  (khella)?
intro: Ammi visnaga has health benefits such as treating respiratory issues, relieving eye strain, and alleviating menstrual cramps. Its medicinal constituents include furanochromones, flavonoids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its pharmacological activities. The herb is prepared in various forms, including capsules, tablets, and tinctures, and can also be consumed as a tea or infusion. However, khella may cause gastrointestinal upset, allergic reactions, or interact with certain medications when taken in excess. To ensure safe use, it is recommended to consult with a healthcare professional before using this herb, especially if you have any underlying medical conditions or are taking prescribed medications.
benefits: Ammi visnaga, also known as khella, has health benefits such as lowering blood pressure and reducing fluid retention. The plant's seeds contain a compound called visnagen, which has been shown to inhibit the production of aldosterone, a hormone that regulates electrolyte balance. Studies have demonstrated that khella extract can be effective in treating conditions like edema, hypertension, and kidney stones, making it a valuable natural remedy for various health issues.
constituents: Ammi visnaga, also known as khella, has active constituents such as visnagin and isovisnagin, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The seeds and roots of the plant contain essential oils, including sesquiterpenes and aldehydes, which contribute to its pharmacological effects. These compounds have been traditionally used to treat various conditions, including hypertension, asthma, and kidney stones, due to their vasodilatory and diuretic properties.
preparations: Ammi visnaga, also known as khella, has medicinal preparations such as tinctures and infusions that are used to treat various ailments. The seeds of Ammi visnaga are rich in khellin and cymarin, compounds that have been shown to have anti-inflammatory, bronchodilatory, and cardiotonic properties. These preparations are often used to treat respiratory problems, such as asthma and bronchitis, as well as heart conditions like palpitations and arrhythmias.
side_effects: Improper use of Ammi visnaga, also known as khella, increases the chances of experiencing side effects such as stomach upset, diarrhea, and vomiting. Some individuals may also experience allergic reactions, including hives and itching. Furthermore, taking high doses or prolonged use can lead to kidney damage and electrolyte imbalance. People with pre-existing conditions, such as kidney disease, should consult with a healthcare professional before using khella to minimize potential risks.
precautions: Before using Ammi visnaga, also known as khella, for medicinal purposes, you must take precautions such as consulting a healthcare professional to determine the safe dosage and potential interactions with other medications. Additionally, pregnant and breastfeeding women should avoid using Ammi visnaga as its effects on the fetus and infant are not well-documented. Use with caution in patients with kidney or liver disease.
intro_desc: Ammi visnaga, also known as khella, has health benefits such as reducing anxiety, treating hypertension, and alleviating respiratory issues. Its medicinal constituents, including coumarins and terpenoids, contribute to its therapeutic effects. Traditionally, khella is prepared as an infusion, tincture, or decoction to utilize its medicinal properties. Possible side effects include gastrointestinal upset and interactions with anticoagulant medications. Precautions include consulting a healthcare professional before using khella, particularly for pregnant or breastfeeding individuals and those taking certain medications.
